title: spergula fascicularis
intro: Spergula fascicularis, also known as beach pepperwort or hairy spurry, is a perennial plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Spergula fascicularis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in some herbal remedies, such as salves and creams, to treat skin conditions and wounds.
